info.title: Aptos Contract API
info.description:
REST API for submitting and tracking Aptos Move transactions. Signing is delegated to Circle Programmable Wallets. Wallet IDs and addresses are supplied per-request — the server holds no wallet state in its config.

## Endpoints

- **POST /v1/execute** — Enqueue an entry function transaction (returns `202` with `status: queued`)
- **POST /v1/query** — Call a view function (sync)
- **GET /v1/transactions/{id}** — Poll transaction status
- **GET /v1/transactions/{id}/webhooks** — List webhook delivery attempts for a transaction
- **GET /v1/health** — Health check (no auth; `?deep=1` also pings MySQL)

## Async Submission Model

`POST /v1/execute` returns `202 Accepted` with a `transaction_id` and `status: queued`. A background worker per sender address claims the row (allocating an Aptos sequence number atomically), signs via Circle, and submits to the Aptos node. Use `GET /v1/transactions/{id}` to poll until a terminal status.

### Transaction Lifecycle

1. **queued** — Persisted in MySQL, waiting for a sender-specific worker
2. **processing** — Claimed by a worker; sequence number allocated, signing in progress
3. **submitted** — Sent to the Aptos node; `txn_hash` populated; awaiting confirmation
4. **confirmed** — Successfully committed on-chain (terminal)
5. **failed** — Simulation, submit, or on-chain execution rejected the transaction (terminal)
6. **expired** — Transaction's on-chain expiration passed before confirmation (terminal)

Transient failures (network blips, Circle 5xx, sequence-number mismatch) requeue the row automatically; `attempt_count` is incremented. Only permanent failures reach `failed` or `expired`.

## Authentication

All endpoints except `/v1/health` require an API key. Pass it either as:

- `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`
- `X-API-Key: <API_KEY>`

Comparison uses constant-time equality. Requests without a valid key receive `401 Unauthorized`.

## Idempotency

`POST /v1/execute` accepts an `idempotency_key` (body field) or `Idempotency-Key` (header). If a record with that key already exists, the handler replays the prior response with `X-Idempotency-Replayed: true` in the response headers; no new transaction is created.

## Error Handling

All 4xx and 5xx responses return a JSON body with a single `error` field:

```json
{"error": "description of the problem"}
```

## Arguments

Arguments are untyped JSON values (strings, numbers, arrays). The server resolves Move types by fetching the module ABI from the Aptos node (cached) and handles BCS serialization automatically.

Supported Move types: `address`, `bool`, `u8`–`u256`, `0x1::string::String`, `vector<T>`, `0x1::object::Object<T>`.

## Address Format

Addresses accept short or long hex (e.g. `0x1` or `0x0000...0001`). They're canonicalized to long-form internally so per-sender queues and sequence counters are consistent regardless of input format.

## Request Validation

- Request bodies must be valid JSON with `Content-Type: application/json`
- Request bodies are limited to 1 MB
- Unknown fields in the request body are rejected (`400 Bad Request`)
- Missing required fields return `400 Bad Request` with a descriptive message

tags.name: Execute
tags.description: Submit entry function transactions to any Move contract

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Call view functions on any Move contract

tags.name: Transactions
tags.description: Track transaction status and webhook delivery history

post.description:
Enqueues a transaction calling the specified Move entry function. Arguments are untyped — the server resolves types from the on-chain module ABI and handles BCS serialization. Returns `202 Accepted` with a `transaction_id` for polling; signing and submission happen asynchronously in a per-sender worker.

**Example: Self-paid transfer**
```json
{
  "wallet_id": "c1ab2c3d-e4f5-6789-abcd-ef0123456789",
  "address": "0xSENDER",
  "function_id": "0x1::aptos_account::transfer",
  "type_arguments": [],
  "arguments": ["0xRECIPIENT", "100"],
  "max_gas_amount": 200000
}
```

**Example: Fee-payer (gas sponsorship)**
```json
{
  "wallet_id": "sender-wallet-uuid",
  "address": "0xSENDER",
  "function_id": "0x1::coin::transfer",
  "type_arguments": ["0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin"],
  "arguments": ["0xRECIPIENT", "100"],
  "fee_payer": {
    "wallet_id": "gas-station-wallet-uuid",
    "address": "0xGAS_STATION"
  }
}
```

**Example: With idempotency and webhook**
```json
{
  "wallet_id": "sender-wallet-uuid",
  "address": "0xSENDER",
  "function_id": "0x1::aptos_account::transfer",
  "arguments": ["0xRECIPIENT", "100"],
  "idempotency_key": "invoice-42",
  "webhook_url": "https://your.host/callbacks/txn"
}
```
Poll `/v1/transactions/{transaction_id}` until `status` is `confirmed`, `failed`, or `expired`; the server also POSTs to `webhook_url` (if supplied) when a terminal status is reached.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional idempotency key. Alternative to the `idempotency_key` body field; the body field takes precedence when both are supplied.

202.description: Transaction accepted and queued for submission. If the `X-Idempotency-Replayed` response header is `true`, the body is a replay of a prior response for the same idempotency key and `txn_hash`/`sequence_number` may be populated if the prior transaction has progressed.

X-Idempotency-Replayed.description: Present and set to `true` when this response is a replay of a prior request with the same idempotency key.

400.description: Invalid request (bad function_id format, unknown fields, missing required fields, unsafe webhook URL, address/public-key mismatch, etc.)

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (only when rate limiting is enabled). Honour the `Retry-After` response header.

500.description: Internal server error (failed to persist the queued transaction)

503.description: Execute endpoint is not configured because Circle or Aptos credentials are missing

post.description:
Calls a Move view function and returns the result synchronously. The server resolves Move argument types from the module ABI, BCS-serializes the arguments, and proxies the call to the Aptos node's `/view` endpoint.

**Example: Balance lookup**
```json
{
  "function_id": "0x1::coin::balance",
  "type_arguments": ["0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin"],
  "arguments": ["0xYOUR_ADDRESS"]
}
```

Returns: `{"result": ["10000"]}`

400.description: Invalid request (bad function_id, argument count mismatch, unsupported type, unresolvable ABI, etc.)

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (only when rate limiting is enabled). Honour the `Retry-After` response header.

502.description: Aptos node unreachable or view call returned an error (e.g. on-chain abort).

503.description: Query endpoint is not configured because Aptos credentials are missing

get.description: Returns the server health status. No authentication required. Pass `?deep=1` to also verify MySQL connectivity — useful for readiness probes.

parameters.name: deep
parameters.description: When set to `1`, the server additionally pings MySQL and returns 500 if the database is unreachable.

200.description: Server is healthy (and MySQL is reachable when `?deep=1`).

500.description: Server is up but MySQL is unreachable (only returned when `?deep=1` is set).

get.description: Returns the current status and metadata for a previously submitted transaction. Use this endpoint to poll for confirmation after calling `/v1/execute`. Terminal statuses are `confirmed`, `failed`, and `expired`.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (only when rate limiting is enabled). Honour the `Retry-After` response header.

get.description: Returns the persistent webhook outbox entries for a transaction, in creation order. Useful for auditing delivery retries and debugging failed notifications. Returns an empty array if no webhook was configured for the transaction or no deliveries have been attempted yet.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (only when rate limiting is enabled). Honour the `Retry-After` response header.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response returned for all 4xx and 5xx status codes.

ExecuteRequest.description: Request body for enqueuing an entry function transaction. Signing and submission happen asynchronously — a 202 only means the row is persisted.

wallet_id.description: Circle wallet identifier that will sign the transaction.

address.description: Aptos account address corresponding to `wallet_id`. Short or long hex both accepted; canonicalized to long-form internally.

public_key.description: Optional Ed25519 public key (0x-prefixed hex). When provided, the server verifies `authkey(public_key) == address` and seeds its internal public-key cache, saving a Circle round-trip on first use. A mismatch returns 400.

function_id.description: Fully qualified Move function ID in the format `<address>::<module>::<function>`.

type_arguments.description: Move type arguments for generic functions (e.g. `["0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin"]`). Omit or pass `[]` when the function has no type parameters.

arguments.description: Function arguments as untyped JSON values. The server resolves expected Move types from the on-chain ABI and BCS-serializes each value at submit time.

items.description: Argument value — type is inferred from the on-chain ABI

max_gas_amount.description: Optional gas limit override. Defaults to the server's configured `MAX_GAS_AMOUNT`.

webhook_url.description: Optional per-request webhook URL invoked when the transaction reaches a terminal status. Subject to SSRF validation (no private, loopback, or link-local addresses). Takes precedence over the server-wide `WEBHOOK_URL`.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key. If a prior request with the same key exists, its response is replayed with `X-Idempotency-Replayed: true`. The body field takes precedence over the `Idempotency-Key` request header.

FeePayer.description: Optional fee-payer (gas station) wallet that sponsors gas for the transaction. When present, the server assembles a fee-payer transaction and collects two Circle signatures (sender and fee payer).

wallet_id.description: Circle wallet identifier for the fee payer.

address.description: Aptos account address for the fee payer.

public_key.description: Optional Ed25519 public key (0x-prefixed hex) for the fee payer, same semantics as the top-level `public_key`.

db.description: Database connectivity, only present when `?deep=1` was set.

QueryRequest.description: Request body for calling a Move view function (read-only, no transaction submission).

function_id.description: Fully qualified Move view function ID in the format `<address>::<module>::<function>`.

type_arguments.description: Move type arguments for generic view functions. Omit or pass `[]` when the function has no type parameters.

arguments.description: View function arguments as untyped JSON values. The server resolves Move types from the on-chain ABI and BCS-serializes each value.

items.description: Argument value — type is inferred from the on-chain ABI

QueryResponse.description: Response from a successful view function call.

result.description: View function return values as a JSON array. Element types follow the Move return signature (e.g. `["10000"]` for a `u64`).

items.description: Return value — type depends on the Move function signature

SubmitResponse.description: Response returned when a transaction is accepted by `/v1/execute`. For an idempotent replay the body can additionally include `txn_hash` and `sequence_number` if the prior transaction has progressed past the `queued` state.

transaction_id.description: Unique identifier for tracking the transaction via `/v1/transactions/{id}`

status.description: Status of the transaction record. `queued` for a new submission; may reflect later states (e.g. `submitted`, `confirmed`) on an idempotent replay.

txn_hash.description: On-chain transaction hash. Only populated on idempotent replays where the prior submission has already reached `submitted` or later.

sequence_number.description: Aptos account sequence number allocated to the transaction. Only populated on idempotent replays where the prior submission has already been claimed.

TransactionRecord.description: Full record of a transaction enqueued via `/v1/execute`, including its current lifecycle status.

id.description: Unique transaction identifier assigned by the API.

idempotency_key.description: Idempotency key stored with the record, if one was provided on `/v1/execute`.

txn_hash.description: On-chain transaction hash. Populated once the record reaches `submitted`.

sender_address.description: Canonical long-form Aptos address of the signer.

wallet_id.description: Circle wallet identifier that signs the transaction.

fee_payer_wallet_id.description: Circle wallet identifier of the fee payer (only set when `/v1/execute` supplied `fee_payer`).

fee_payer_address.description: Canonical long-form Aptos address of the fee payer (only set when `/v1/execute` supplied `fee_payer`).

payload_json.description: JSON-encoded `{type_arguments, arguments}` payload the submitter rebuilds the entry function from. Stored as a string for audit.

sequence_number.description: Aptos account sequence number allocated at claim time. NULL while the record is `queued`.

max_gas_amount.description: Optional max gas override supplied on `/v1/execute`.

error_message.description: Error message from the most recent failure, if any.

attempt_count.description: Number of times this record has been retried by the submitter.

last_error.description: Last observed retry error (may be transient). Cleared on successful submission.

failure_kind.description: Category of permanent failure. Only set when `status` is `failed` or `expired`.

vm_status.description: Structured VM status from the Aptos `/transactions/simulate` response when simulation produced a reject (e.g. `Move abort ... EINSUFFICIENT_BALANCE`).

created_at.description: When the record was first accepted by the API. Also the per-sender FIFO ordering key.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent state change.

expires_at.description: Transaction expiration time (`created_at + TXN_EXPIRATION_SECONDS`). The submitter marks the record `expired` if this passes before submission.

WebhookDeliveryRecord.description: A single webhook delivery attempt recorded in the persistent outbox. Field names are serialized from the struct field names (Go default), not snake_case.

TransactionID.description: ID of the transaction the delivery is for.

Payload.description: JSON-encoded payload that was (or will be) POSTed to the URL.

Attempts.description: Number of delivery attempts so far.

LastAttemptAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent delivery attempt.

LastError.description: Error from the most recent failed attempt, if any.

NextRetryAt.description: When the worker is next allowed to retry. Exponential backoff, capped at 5 minutes between attempts.

CreatedAt.description: When the delivery record was created.

bearerAuth.description: API key passed as an Authorization bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`.

apiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
apiKeyAuth.description: API key passed as the raw value of the `X-API-Key` header (no `Bearer ` prefix).
